figures.caption: Fig. 1. Problem formulation. Given unpaired sets of labeled simulation and unlabeled real samples, our framework DRUM generates labeled pseudo-real samples for training LiDAR segmentation.

Fig. 6. Effect of mixed training using our pseudo-real samples. We show scores of mIoU (%) on image-based 19-class semantic segmentation. We mix real data at ratios of 1%, 20%, 50%, and 100% with our pseudoreal data during training.
TABLE IV QUANTITATIVE COMPARISON OF SIM2REAL METHODS ON SEMANTIC SEGMENTATION (SYNLIDAR → SEMANTICKITTI).

Fig. 5. Qualitative comparison of semantic segmentation results. We show the results of the image-based method on the representation of images (top row) and point clouds (bottom row). Our approach significantly improves ■ car detection (black circles) compared to the baseline.

Fig. 2. Overview of Sim2Real translation by DRUM. (a) The unconditional generation process of diffusion models is conditioned by the masked guidance with the simulation sample y. (b) In masked guidance, we first generate the raydrop-aware mt mask from the tentative Tweedie sample x̂t and then compute the sim–real discrepancy based on the pseudoinverse method [19]. The operator H corrupts the reflectance modality.
